page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.large is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with a single thread per core. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside constrained multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves an average rating in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores. Single-threaded tasks such as bzip3 decompression show strong relative performance at 14.33 GB/sec. However, multi-core benchmarks are weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3401.18 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3232.0. Memory bandwidth is mixed, registering average speeds for large-block read operations but falling to weak levels for small-block reads and large-block writes.

The m8gb.large represents a cost-efficient entry point for ARM-based general-purpose workloads, offering dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. However, users face clear performance tradeoffs. Cryptographic and extended instruction sets perform poorly, as evidenced by low Passmark CPU encryption scores. Database operations on Passmark show average performance, while Redis SET operations are weak at 4.59 million operations per second. Given the 2-core limit and lack of local storage, this instance is suited for lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and single-threaded utility tasks rather than intensive multi-threaded applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated arm64 physical cores powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, a 36 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps, operating on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage. Performance benchmarks reveal a clear tradeoff: single-core execution is average, highlighted by a strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 14.33 GB/sec, whereas multi-core and memory write speeds are weak. Passmark database operations perform at an average level, but cryptographic operations and multi-threaded processing are poor. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic text processing, and single-threaded utility tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.large is a general-purpose arm64 instance with 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight utility workloads.

tagline: An entry-level, dedicated arm64 instance featuring balanced memory-to-core allocation for lightweight general-purpose workloads and single-threaded utility tasks.
bullet_points: Two dedicated arm64 physical cores utilizing AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors
8.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance of 14.33 GB/sec
Weak multi-core and memory write speeds indicating limited parallel processing capacity
Suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded utility tasks
